explanation: The property states that globally (G), if the system is in Standby Mode (StandbyMode) AND a patient is not yet connected (!patientConnected) AND patient ventilation has not started (!ventilating), THEN it must be possible to adjust ventilation parameters (ventilationParamsAdjustable) AND it must be possible to enter user-controlled alarm thresholds (enterAlarmThresholds). This captures the requirement that parameter adjustments are possible under the specified conditions before patient connection and ventilation.

The property states that it must Always (G) be true that if the system is in 'StandbyMode' AND has not yet been 'patientConnected' OR 'ventilating' (i.e., patient connection and ventilation has not started), THEN 'ventilationParamsAdjustable' AND 'enterAlarmThresholds' must be true. This captures the requirement that parameter adjustment is possible in Standby Mode before the patient is connected and ventilation begins.

The property states that globally, if the system is in Standby Mode, then it must be possible to adjust all user-controlled parameters for ventilation and alarms. The 'before connecting to the patient and starting patient ventilation' clause is implicitly handled, as Standby Mode is described as a state where 'ventilation is off' and the system is 'ready for ventilating a patient', implying it precedes patient connection and ventilation initiation. Therefore, any moment in Standby Mode is by definition 'before' these events.

The property states that globally, if the system is in Standby Mode (bool_exp1), then it will eventually be possible to adjust both ventilation and alarm parameters (part of bool_exp3). The crucial 'before' condition is encoded by asserting that at the time of adjustment, the patient must not yet be connected and patient ventilation must not yet have started (also part of bool_exp3). This is captured by the template 'G (bool_exp1 -> (F bool_exp3))'.

The property states that globally, if the system is in Standby Mode, it must eventually be possible to adjust ventilation and alarm parameters. Crucially, this adjustment must occur at a point in time before the patient is connected or patient ventilation begins. This is captured by ensuring that when the parameters are adjustable, the conditions 'patientConnected' and 'ventilating' are false.

The property states that globally, whenever the system is in 'StandbyMode', it implies that both 'ventilation parameters are adjustable' and 'user-controlled alarm thresholds can be entered'. The phrase 'before connecting to the patient and starting patient ventilation' describes the temporal context of the 'StandbyMode' itself, indicating that this mode is the designated time for such adjustments prior to patient connection and ventilation. Therefore, ensuring adjustability within 'StandbyMode' satisfies the 'before' condition without requiring an explicit LTL temporal operator beyond the implication.

The property states that globally, if the system is in 'StandbyMode' AND the patient is NOT yet connected (represented by '!patientConnected') AND patient ventilation has NOT yet started (represented by '!ventilating'), THEN it must be possible to adjust ventilation parameters ('ventilationParamsAdjustable') AND enter alarm thresholds ('enterAlarmThresholds'). This captures the 'before connecting to the patient and starting patient ventilation' aspect by making the adjustability conditional on these events not having occurred yet, while operating in Standby Mode.

The property states that in 'Standby Mode', parameter adjustments are possible 'before' the patient is connected and ventilation starts. This is best captured by an invariant property (Globally true) that holds when the system is in 'Standby Mode' AND the conditions for patient connection and ventilation initiation have NOT yet been met. If these conditions are met, the property no longer needs to hold within the specified scope. Therefore, the LTL template 'G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)' is chosen, where 'bool_exp1' represents 'being in Standby Mode AND not (patient connected AND ventilating)', and 'bool_exp3' represents 'ventilation parameters adjustable AND alarm settings changed (implying adjustability)'.

The property states that globally, if the system is in 'StandbyMode' AND the 'patientConnected' state is false AND 'ventilating' is false, then it must always be true that 'ventilationParamsAdjustable' are true AND 'enterAlarmThresholds' is true. This ensures that parameter adjustments are possible in Standby Mode before the patient is connected and ventilation begins.

The property states that globally, if the system is in Standby Mode (bool_exp2), then it must not be connected to the patient and patient ventilation must not be started (bool_exp3). This captures the requirement that parameter adjustments, which are possible in Standby Mode as per its definition, must occur 'before' connecting to the patient and starting ventilation, by ensuring these latter events do not happen while in Standby Mode.
